title: The Dragon's Tribute
premise: For a decade, the town of Oakhaven has paid a yearly tribute of gold and cattle to the dragon Vortharax to avoid destruction. Now, a band of self-proclaimed heroes arrives claiming they can slay the beast for good, but their demands for payment match the tribute's cost. The council must decide: continue the uneasy peace or gamble on the strangers — while the dragon's true motives remain shrouded.
setting: Oakhaven, a prosperous but fearful town at the foot of the Dragon's Spine mountains. The stone-walled council chamber buzzes with tension as the annual tribute deadline looms in three days. Townsfolk are divided between tradition and hope, and a chill in the air hints at winter — or the dragon's displeasure.
goal: As the town council, decide whether to pay the dragon's tribute or fund the heroes' quest. Success means ensuring Oakhaven's safety and prosperity for the next year; failure leads to destruction by the dragon or financial ruin from the heroes' demands. The scenario ends when the council makes a binding decision.
gm_notes: The dragon Vortharax is an ancient wyrm who hoards not just treasure but knowledge; it has been subtly manipulating the town's economy and politics to keep them dependent. The heroes are actually a disgraced noble, a scoundrel, and a charlatan, but they have genuine dragon-lore and a risky plan involving a sleeping curse. Introduce complications: a spy for the dragon in town, a hidden treasury shortfall, and a child who claims to speak with the dragon. Pacing: first session is debate, second session reveals hidden info, third session forces a vote.

roles.name: Mayor Elara Thornwood
roles.description: The pragmatic mayor who has overseen tribute payments for six years. She fears change but secretly wants freedom from the dragon.
roles.private_info: You know that the town's treasury is nearly empty — another tribute will push Oakhaven into debt. You also received a cryptic note from the dragon last night: 'The new flames are not mine.'

roles.name: Captain Aldric Vane
roles.description: Grizzled head of the town guard, weary but honorable. He distrusts the heroes and prefers the devil they know.
roles.private_info: You discovered the heroes' horses bear the brand of a known outlaw gang. You suspect they plan to extort the town and flee. You have a secret stash of weapons hidden in the old watchtower.

roles.name: Merchant Lira Gray
roles.description: Wealthy merchant council member who profits from tribute contracts. She outwardly supports the heroes but has her own agenda.
roles.private_info: You have a secret deal with the heroes to split any treasure from the dragon's hoard. You also owe a gambling debt to a rival merchant who is the dragon's secret servant.

roles.name: Sage Orin Moth
roles.description: Old, eccentric scholar who studies dragon lore. He believes the tribute has kept a worse evil at bay.
roles.private_info: You have deciphered an ancient prophecy: if the dragon's tribute is refused, a greater terror will awaken from the mountains. You also know the dragon's true name, which could bind it, but using it would cost a life.

npcs.name: Captain Zara Steelblade
npcs.description: Bold leader of the heroes, scarred and confident, carrying a massive sword. She speaks of dragon weakness with certainty.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Zara is a disgraced knight who fled her order after a failed dragon hunt. She genuinely wants to kill Vortharax to restore her honor, but she's desperate and her plan has only a 30% chance of success.

npcs.name: Thorn Irons
npcs.description: Quiet, hooded hero who rarely speaks. Claims to be a ranger from the north.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Thorn is actually an assassin hired by a neighboring kingdom to destabilize Oakhaven by provoking the dragon. He will sabotage the heroes if they succeed.

npcs.name: Vortharax
npcs.description: The ancient red dragon, never seen in full, but its voice echoes from the mountain. It demands tribute but also offers cryptic advice.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Vortharax is dying from a magical wasting curse. It collects tribute to find a cure, and it has been subtly guiding the town to prosperity to attract scholars who might help. It fears the heroes because their attack might accelerate its death.

npcs.name: Mira Thornwood
npcs.description: Mayor Elara's teenage daughter, who has been secretly meeting a stranger in the woods.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Mira has been visited by a humanoid form of Vortharax, who has been teaching her magic in exchange for her promise to end the tribute peacefully. She believes the dragon is misunderstood.
